meta.name: The Orchard at Low Tide
meta.description: A brackish civic orchard must survive a failed moon-cistern, a private graft monopoly, and a season charter that treats shared memory as somebody else's property.
meta.author: Clean-room reference author

attributes.name: Graft
attributes.description: Join living systems without collapsing their differences.

attributes.name: Current
attributes.description: Read water, weather, route, and timing under pressure.

attributes.name: Memory
attributes.description: Preserve provenance, testimony, and the reasons a settlement changed.

attributes.name: Patience
attributes.description: Hold a public process together when urgency rewards private command.

progressionTiers.name: Survey
progressionTiers.flavorText: The orchard learns what is failing and who has been excluded from the count.

progressionTiers.name: Descent
progressionTiers.flavorText: The settlement enters the moon-cistern and tests the ownership story attached to it.

progressionTiers.name: Renewal
progressionTiers.flavorText: The orchard decides which living systems and records the next season inherits.

challenges.name: Count the Brackish Wells
challenges.description: Survey every public and private well before the low-tide window closes, including the households the old water map treats as temporary.

mechanicChecks.name: Read the Waterline
mechanicChecks.description: Distinguish seasonal salt from structural failure without declaring every unfamiliar well contaminated.

success.narrative: The public count includes the hidden wells and the people who maintain them.

stateEffects.reason: The census reveals usable reserve.
The complete count enters public custody.

partial.narrative: The count reaches the main basins but leaves one household's well inside the private annex.

stateEffects.reason: The omission is recorded rather than erased.

failure.narrative: The tide returns before the map can be contested, preserving the private count as operational truth.

stateEffects.reason: The private map becomes more indispensable.

challenges.name: Negotiate the Graft Exchange
challenges.description: Open the private graft catalogue without destroying the expertise and living cultures on which the orchard already depends.

mechanicChecks.name: Separate Custody from Ownership
mechanicChecks.description: Document who keeps cultures alive, who can copy them, and which claims exist only because no alternative was funded.

success.narrative: The catalogue remains maintained, but its cultures and methods become forkable under public custody.

stateEffects.reason: Several graft lines enter lawful circulation.
The orchard gains a replacement path.
The exchange accepts bounded public review.

partial.narrative: The catalogue opens, but one living culture remains tied to a single custodian.

stateEffects.reason: Some graft lines circulate.

failure.narrative: The hearing becomes a seizure; custodians hide their cultures and the orchard loses both trust and redundancy.

stateEffects.reason: The custody record fragments.

challenges.name: Dive the Moon Cistern
challenges.description: Enter the flooded cistern that once balanced the orchard and recover its route memory before another tide seals it.

mechanicChecks.name: Cross the Silt Clock
mechanicChecks.description: Move through current, darkness, and sediment while keeping route observations attributable to the people who made them.

success.narrative: The cistern route is reopened with its old maintenance testimony and its present danger intact.

stateEffects.reason: The restored route reaches a second basin.
The route returns with provenance.

partial.narrative: The route opens, but the deepest observations survive only through one diver's memory.

stateEffects.reason: The basin is reachable.
One witness becomes indispensable.

failure.narrative: The tide seals the descent and the orchard must ration against a map it still cannot audit.

stateEffects.reason: The reachable reserve narrows.

challenges.name: Replant the Ninth Orchard
challenges.description: Use the recovered water and opened graft catalogue to establish a field no single household can close.

mechanicChecks.name: Join Water, Root, and Charter
mechanicChecks.description: Plant for ecological survival while retaining several lawful ways to maintain, copy, refuse, and revise the field.

success.narrative: The ninth field takes root under plural maintenance and a published replacement plan.

stateEffects.reason: Several living lines survive together.
No single household owns the maintenance path.

partial.narrative: The field lives, but one water gate or graft culture remains private.

stateEffects.reason: The field is viable but narrow.

failure.narrative: The planting hardens scarcity into a new monopoly and consumes the reserve meant to create alternatives.

stateEffects.reason: The failed solution narrows future choice.

challenges.name: Publish the Next Season
challenges.description: Write the changed water, graft, memory, and maintenance order into a charter later residents can inspect and refuse.

mechanicChecks.name: Write a Revisable Season
mechanicChecks.description: Record evidence, dissent, replacement paths, and review dates without turning the emergency settlement into timeless law.

success.narrative: The season charter names its evidence, limits, dissent, and expiration. The orchard enters the next tide without a hidden owner.

stateEffects.reason: The charter enters plural custody.
The changed order is recorded with provenance.

partial.narrative: The charter is published, but one review power remains attached to the emergency custodians.

stateEffects.reason: The record exists despite unresolved authority.

failure.narrative: The orchard survives, but its new order remains private practice rather than reviewable law.

stateEffects.reason: Unwritten custom becomes another gate.

narrativeEvents.title: The next tide is public
narrativeEvents.text: The orchard survives with a changed map of water, rootstock, memory, and authority. Later holders inherit the receipt rather than a clean founding myth.

opening.narrativeText: Low tide exposes the cracked wells and the locked graft annex at the same hour. The first priority will shape what the settlement calls urgent.

options.label: Open the wells first
options.description: Protect immediate water access while accepting that the private catalogue may close.

options.label: Open the ledgers first
options.description: Preserve evidence and replacement paths while accepting a tighter rationing window.

stateDefinitions.label: Field Census Published
stateDefinitions.description: Whether every known well and field has entered public custody.

stateDefinitions.label: Monopoly Debt
stateDefinitions.description: Present survival tied to one private map, culture, gate, or custodian.

stateDefinitions.label: Public Memory
stateDefinitions.description: Provenance, testimony, and revision history in shared custody.

stateDefinitions.label: Season Charter
stateDefinitions.description: The current custody status of the orchard's operating order.

stateDefinitions.label: Seed Diversity
stateDefinitions.description: Independent living lines and replacement paths.

observations: the hidden wells entered the census
